title: Magnetic Position and Orientation Tracking System
authors: Raab, Frederick H.; Blood, Ernest B.; Steiner, Terry O.; Jones, Herbert R.
venue: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
notes: Seminal formalization of quasi-static magnetic-dipole 6-DOF tracking. DOI inferred from IEEE TAES AES-15(5); page range 709-718 confirmed via multiple indexes.

title: Electromagnetic Tracking in Medicine—A Review of Technology, Validation, and Applications
authors: Franz, Alfred M.; Haidegger, Tamás; Birkfellner, Wolfgang; Cleary, Kevin; Peters, Terry M.; Maier-Hein, Lena
notes: The definitive modern review of medical EMT. DOI and citation verified via PubMed/dblp.

title: Design and application of an assessment protocol for electromagnetic tracking systems
authors: Hummel, Johann B.; Bax, Michael R.; Figl, Michael L.; Kang, Yan; Maurer, Calvin R.; Birkfellner, Wolfgang W.; Bergmann, Helmar; Shahidi, Ramin
keywords: assessment protocol; accuracy; phantom; Aurora; microBIRD
notes: Standardized phantom/protocol for comparable EMT accuracy assessment. DOI verified. Volume/issue/pages to re-confirm against Wiley record.

title: A Novel Method for Nonfluoroscopic Catheter-Based Electroanatomical Mapping of the Heart: In Vitro and In Vivo Accuracy Results
authors: Gepstein, Lior; Hayam, Gal; Ben-Haim, Shlomo A.
notes: Foundational clinical paper underlying the Biosense CARTO system. PMID 9118532 verified.

title: Systematic distortions in magnetic position digitizers
authors: Birkfellner, Wolfgang; Watzinger, Franz; Wanschitz, Felix; Ewers, Rolf; Bergmann, Helmar
keywords: distortion; metal; eddy current; ferromagnetic; characterization
notes: Early systematic characterization of field distortion in magnetic digitizers. DOI/pages to re-confirm against Wiley record; citation widely reported as Med Phys 25(11):2242-2248.

title: A survey of electromagnetic position tracker calibration techniques
keywords: calibration; distortion correction; polynomial fit; interpolation
notes: Survey of EMT calibration/distortion-correction methods (interpolation, polynomial, shape functions). DOI verified; volume/issue/pages to re-confirm.

title: A new method for magnetic position and orientation tracking
authors: Paperno, Eugene; Sasada, Ichiro; Leonovich, Eduard
notes: Rotating quasi-static field method; alternative excitation/decoding strategy. DOI to re-confirm against IEEE record.

title: 3-D Magnetic Tracking of a Single Subminiature Coil with a Large 2-D Array of Uniaxial Transmitters
keywords: transmitter array; subminiature coil; subarray; computation
notes: Array-of-transmitters architecture with subarray tracking for speed. DOI/article-id to re-confirm against IEEE record.

title: Magnetic Sensors and Their Applications
authors: Lenz, James E.; Edelstein, Alan S.
keywords: magnetic sensors; search coil; fluxgate; Hall; AMR; GMR; MTJ; review
notes: Canonical review comparing all major magnetic sensor families incl. search coil, fluxgate, Hall, AMR, GMR, magnetic tunnel junctions. Vol 6, pp 631-649 confirmed; DOI to re-confirm against IEEE record.

title: Magnetoresistive sensor detectivity: A comparative analysis
authors: Davies, J. E.; Watts, J. D.; Novotny, J.; Huang, D.; Eames, P. G.
keywords: detectivity; 1/f noise; GMR; TMR; MTJ; die area; fundamental limit
notes: Comparative noise/detectivity analysis of GMR vs TMR(MTJ); finds detectivity scales with die area and converges across types when normalized for linear range. DOI verified via AIP.

title: Electromagnetic tracking in the clinical environment
authors: Yaniv, Ziv; Wilson, Emmanuel; Lindisch, David; Cleary, Kevin
keywords: clinical EMT; accuracy; distortion; interventional; assessment
notes: Clinical-environment characterization of EMT accuracy and distortion. Citation 36(3):876-892, DOI 10.1118/1.3075829, PMID 19378748 verified.

title: Aurora Electromagnetic Tracking System — Sensors (5DOF/6DOF, micro 0.3 mm)
notes: Vendor source for medical-grade sensor form factors (e.g., micro 5DOF ~0.3 mm dia). Vendor-reported; access year approximate.

title: IEC 60601-1 Medical electrical equipment — Part 1: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance
keywords: medical safety; leakage current; means of protection; isolation; Type BF/CF
notes: Governing safety standard for medical electrical equipment. Patient leakage limits e.g. Type CF 10 uA normal / 50 uA single-fault; two Means of Protection required. Edition history per IEC webstore.

title: IEC 60601-1-2 Medical electrical equipment — Part 1-2: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance — Collateral standard: Electromagnetic disturbances — Requirements and tests
keywords: EMC; electromagnetic compatibility; risk-based; essential performance; immunity
notes: EMC collateral standard. 4th ed (2014) shifted to risk/intended-use-based tests by environment (professional healthcare / home / special). Edition 4.1 = Amendment 1 (2020).

title: The Art of Electronics, 3rd Edition
keywords: analog; low-noise amplifier; op-amp noise; instrumentation amplifier; reference
notes: Standard reference for low-noise analog design (amplifier voltage/current noise, noise matching, instrumentation amps). ISBN 978-0521809269.

title: Analog-to-Digital Converter Survey and Analysis
venue: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
keywords: ADC; survey; resolution vs sampling rate; jitter; thermal limit; figure of merit
notes: Canonical ADC survey; establishes resolution-vs-rate trends and limiting mechanisms (thermal at low rate, ~1 bit/octave fall-off at high rate; aperture jitter). DOI 10.1109/49.761034 verified.

title: IEEE Standard for Terminology and Test Methods for Analog-to-Digital Converters
keywords: ADC test; ENOB; SINAD; SNR; FFT test; terminology
notes: Defines ADC error terminology and FFT-based test methods (ENOB from SINAD, etc.). Multiple editions (2000/2010/2023); cite the edition used in a given measurement.

title: Our Technology — AC Electromagnetic Tracking
notes: Vendor self-description of AC electromagnetic tracking; used only for vendor-attributed claims. Access year approximate; treat as living document.
